name: Palworld (Windows / UE4SS — Experimental)
author: ops@refx.example
description: EXPERIMENTAL: Palworld's WINDOWS dedicated server run under Proton/Wine with the RE-UE4SS mod loader bootstrapped, so Windows-only server-side mods can load. The native Linux Palworld build cannot load mods; this egg trades stability for moddability. Windows-via-Proton is the flakiest egg class — use it for testing, not production.
startupDetect: Setting breakpad minidump|Running Palworld dedicated server

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
# ReFx steamcmd shim — the steamcmd:proton install image ships no `steamcmd` on
# PATH. Bootstrap SteamCMD and expose it so the egg's `steamcmd ...` calls
# resolve (otherwise the game server never downloads).
if ! command -v steamcmd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamcmd
  curl -sSL https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz | tar zxf - -C /mnt/server/steamcmd || { echo "ERROR: failed to fetch steamcmd"; exit 1; }
  chmod +x /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh /mnt/server/steamcmd/linux32/steamcmd 2>/dev/null || true
  printf '#!/bin/sh\nexec /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh "$@"\n' > /usr/local/bin/steamcmd
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/steamcmd
fi
set -uo pipefail
# --- ReFx SteamCMD update safety (injected) --------------------------------
# Guards every Steam update: a stuck/partial SteamCMD run (state 0x6, "Missing
# file permissions", ENOSPC, transient failure) must NOT be reported as success
# — that strands the server on a stale build ("wrong build" on join). We key off
# the app's manifest StateFlags: exactly "4" == fully installed & current.
REFX_APPID="2394010"
refx_steam_manifest() { echo "/mnt/server/steamapps/appmanifest_${1}.acf"; }
refx_steam_installed() { local m; m="$(refx_steam_manifest "$1")"; [ -f "$m" ] && grep -Eq '"StateFlags"[[:space:]]+"4"' "$m"; }
refx_steam_heal() { # drop a stale/partial manifest so this update re-acquires cleanly
  local m; m="$(refx_steam_manifest "$1")"
  if [ -f "$m" ] && ! refx_steam_installed "$1"; then
    echo "[refx] steam app $1: a previous install left a stale manifest (not fully-installed) — clearing so this update re-acquires the build cleanly."
    rm -f "$m" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  rm -rf "/mnt/server/steamapps/downloading/$1" "/mnt/server/steamapps/temp/$1" 2>/dev/null || true
}
refx_steam_verify() { # HARD-FAIL if the app is not fully installed after the update
  if refx_steam_installed "$1"; then
    echo "[refx] steam app $1 is on the current build (SteamCMD confirmed fully installed)."
    return 0
  fi
  echo "[refx] ERROR: SteamCMD did not finish updating app $1 — the game files were NOT"
  echo "[refx] changed, so the server is still on its previous build and clients may see a"
  echo "[refx] 'wrong build' / version-mismatch error on join."
  echo "[refx][diag] install uid:gid = $(id -u 2>/dev/null):$(id -g 2>/dev/null)"
  df -h /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][disk] /' || true
  ls -ln /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head -20 || true
  # Clear the stale manifest so the NEXT update self-heals (clean re-acquire).
  rm -f "$(refx_steam_manifest "$1")" 2>/dev/null || true
  echo "[refx] Cleared the stale manifest — run Update again and it will re-download the"
  echo "[refx] current build. Your world/config under the data dir are untouched."
  exit 1
}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
refx_steam_heal "$REFX_APPID"

mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamapps
cd /mnt/server
# EXPERIMENTAL: install the WINDOWS Palworld dedicated server build (the Linux
# build can NOT load UE4SS). `+@sSteamCmdForcePlatformType windows` forces the
# Windows depots on a Linux host (same pattern as abiotic-factor / conan-exiles /
# v-rising). The base build is anonymous — no owning Steam account needed.
WIN_EXE=/mnt/server/Pal/Binaries/Win64/PalServer-Win64-Shipping.exe
STEAM_LOG="$(mktemp 2>/dev/null || echo /tmp/refx-steam.log)"
steam_win_update() {
  steamcmd +@sSteamCmdForcePlatformType windows +force_install_dir /mnt/server +login anonymous +app_update 2394010 validate +quit 2>&1 | tee "$STEAM_LOG"
}
refx_make_writable() {
  # Installs run as ROOT, but a reinstall leaves game files owned by the game uid
  # (the runtime chowns the data dir to it on start). SteamCMD does an explicit
  # owner-writability pre-check and bails with "Missing file permissions" — it is
  # NOT a real perms wall on a root install, and disk is not the constraint here.
  # Re-assert root ownership + writability before every attempt: the exact,
  # documented fix the Linux palworld egg uses for this same app.
  chown -R root:root /mnt/server 2>/dev/null || true
  chmod -R u+rwX /mnt/server 2>/dev/null || true
}
refx_make_writable
# Skip the SteamCMD download when the Windows build is already intact: forcing the
# Windows platform to RE-validate on a reinstall is unreliable ("Missing
# configuration" / "Missing file permissions"), and re-downloading ~6GB is not
# needed just to re-run the install script (UE4SS / ini / launcher). Delete
# Pal/Binaries/Win64 to force a fresh fetch.
if [ -f "$WIN_EXE" ]; then
  echo "[refx] Windows Palworld build already present — skipping the SteamCMD download."
else
  echo "[refx] Fetching the Windows Palworld build via SteamCMD (forced Windows platform)..."
  steam_win_update || true
fi
# GROUND-TRUTH verify: the Windows server EXE must actually exist. Forcing the
# Windows platform on Linux can fail on a reinstall with "Missing configuration"
# (stale appmanifest/appcache) or "Missing file permissions" (game-uid-owned
# leftovers) — and either can leave a manifest that CLAIMS installed while no
# files were fetched, which is why the manifest-only refx_steam_verify reported a
# false success. So we check the real binary; on a miss we wipe the Steam app
# state, re-assert permissions, and retry ONE clean forced-Windows acquire.
# Customer world saves under Pal/Saved are never touched.
if [ ! -f "$WIN_EXE" ]; then
  echo "[refx] Windows build not present after the first SteamCMD run (forced-platform 'Missing configuration' / 'Missing file permissions' on a reinstall) — clearing stale Steam state, re-asserting permissions, and retrying a clean acquire; this re-downloads the game. Pal/Saved untouched."
  rm -f /mnt/server/steamapps/appmanifest_2394010.acf 2>/dev/null || true
  rm -rf /mnt/server/steamapps/downloading /mnt/server/steamapps/temp /mnt/server/steamapps/shadercache 2>/dev/null || true
  rm -rf /home/container/Steam/appcache /root/Steam/appcache /mnt/server/Steam/appcache 2>/dev/null || true
  refx_make_writable
  steam_win_update || true
fi
if [ ! -f "$WIN_EXE" ]; then
  echo "[refx] ERROR: the Windows Palworld server binary is still missing after a clean retry."
  echo "[refx] SteamCMD did not fetch the Windows depot. Forcing the Windows platform on"
  echo "[refx] Linux commonly reports 'Missing configuration' (stale manifest/appcache) or"
  echo "[refx] 'Missing file permissions' (game-uid-owned leftovers on reinstall / low disk)."
  echo "[refx] Last SteamCMD output:"
  tail -n 25 "$STEAM_LOG" 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][steam] /' || true
  echo "[refx][diag] install uid:gid = $(id -u 2>/dev/null):$(id -g 2>/dev/null)"
  df -h /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][disk] /' || true
  ls -ln /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head -20 || true
  ls -la /mnt/server/Pal/Binaries 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head || true
  exit 1
fi
echo "[refx] Windows Palworld build present ($WIN_EXE) — SteamCMD verified by binary, not just the manifest."

# ---- Bootstrap RE-UE4SS (the Windows-only server-side mod loader) ----------
# UE4SS ships a `dwmapi.dll` proxy that injects next to the game exe on load.
# The STABLE v3.0.1 zip is a FLAT layout (dwmapi.dll + Mods/ at the archive
# root); the community-recommended `experimental-latest` build ships a `ue4ss/`
# folder (with Mods/ inside) and is what CURRENT Palworld (UE5.1, post-0.4.15)
# actually needs. We pin the experimental-latest TAG and resolve its rolling
# asset name from the GitHub API, falling back to the pinned stable asset if that
# lookup fails. Both layouts are handled below; either way dwmapi.dll must land
# in Pal/Binaries/Win64 next to PalServer-Win64-Shipping.exe.
WIN64=/mnt/server/Pal/Binaries/Win64
if [ ! -d "$WIN64" ]; then
  echo "[refx] ERROR: $WIN64 is missing — the Windows Palworld build did not install; cannot bootstrap UE4SS."
  exit 1
fi
UE4SS_TAG="experimental-latest"
UE4SS_STABLE_URL="https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases/download/v3.0.1/UE4SS_v3.0.1.zip"
UE4SS_ZIP="/tmp/refx-ue4ss.zip"

# Extract a zip even if the image ships no `unzip` (fall back to python3 / bsdtar,
# then apt-get as a last resort).
refx_unzip() { # $1=zip  $2=destdir
  if command -v unzip >/dev/null 2>&1; then unzip -o "$1" -d "$2" >/dev/null; return $?; fi
  if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then python3 -m zipfile -e "$1" "$2"; return $?; fi
  if command -v bsdtar >/dev/null 2>&1; then bsdtar -xf "$1" -C "$2"; return $?; fi
  apt-get update >/dev/null 2>&1 && apt-get install -y unzip >/dev/null 2>&1 && unzip -o "$1" -d "$2" >/dev/null
}

# Resolve the experimental asset URL (no jq): first UE4SS_v*.zip that is NOT a
# zDEV/debug build.
UE4SS_URL="$(curl -sSL "https://api.github.com/repos/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases/tags/${UE4SS_TAG}" \
  | grep -Eo '"browser_download_url":[[:space:]]*"[^"]*"' \
  | sed -E 's/.*"(https[^"]+)".*/\1/' \
  | grep -E '/UE4SS_v[^/]*\.zip$' \
  | grep -viE 'zdev' \
  | head -n1)"
if [ -z "$UE4SS_URL" ]; then
  echo "[refx] UE4SS: could not resolve the experimental asset from GitHub — falling back to the pinned stable build."
  UE4SS_URL="$UE4SS_STABLE_URL"
fi
echo "[refx] UE4SS: downloading $UE4SS_URL"
if ! curl -sSL -o "$UE4SS_ZIP" "$UE4SS_URL"; then
  echo "[refx] ERROR: failed to download UE4SS from $UE4SS_URL"
  exit 1
fi
if ! refx_unzip "$UE4SS_ZIP" "$WIN64"; then
  echo "[refx] ERROR: failed to unzip UE4SS into $WIN64 (no unzip/python3/bsdtar available?)."
  exit 1
fi
rm -f "$UE4SS_ZIP" 2>/dev/null || true

# Hard-fail if the proxy DLL isn't present next to the exe — without it UE4SS
# never injects and the whole point of this egg is gone.
if [ ! -f "$WIN64/dwmapi.dll" ]; then
  echo "[refx] ERROR: UE4SS dwmapi.dll is not present in $WIN64 after extraction — the loader will not inject."
  ls -la "$WIN64" 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head -40
  exit 1
fi

# Find where UE4SS's files landed: flat (UE4SS-settings.ini in Win64) vs the
# experimental `ue4ss/` (or `UE4SS/`) folder.
UE_DIR="$WIN64"
[ -d "$WIN64/ue4ss" ] && UE_DIR="$WIN64/ue4ss"
[ -d "$WIN64/UE4SS" ] && UE_DIR="$WIN64/UE4SS"
UE_INI="$UE_DIR/UE4SS-settings.ini"
mkdir -p "$UE_DIR/Mods"

# Palworld-under-wine caveats (UE4SS v3 release notes + Palworld modding wiki):
#  - headless: force dx11 so the GUI console (absent under xvfb) can't wedge init
#  - bUseUObjectArrayCache=false avoids a known startup crash on some builds
if [ -f "$UE_INI" ]; then
  if grep -qiE '^[; ]*GraphicsAPI[[:space:]]*=' "$UE_INI"; then
    sed -i -E 's/^[; ]*GraphicsAPI[[:space:]]*=.*/GraphicsAPI = dx11/' "$UE_INI" 2>/dev/null || true
  else
    printf '\nGraphicsAPI = dx11\n' >> "$UE_INI"
  fi
  if grep -qiE '^[; ]*bUseUObjectArrayCache[[:space:]]*=' "$UE_INI"; then
    sed -i -E 's/^[; ]*bUseUObjectArrayCache[[:space:]]*=.*/bUseUObjectArrayCache = false/' "$UE_INI" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
fi
# A pre-3.0 UE4SS used xinput1_3.dll as its proxy; a lingering copy CRASHES the
# game alongside the new dwmapi proxy. Remove it.
rm -f "$WIN64/xinput1_3.dll" 2>/dev/null || true
echo "[refx] UE4SS installed. Mods directory: $UE_DIR/Mods (drop a server-side mod here and reinstall/restart)."

# --- Probe: which Windows runner does this image expose to us? --------------
# The steamcmd:proton yolk sets wine/Proton up in its OWN entrypoint, which the
# agent bypasses (it execs the startup argv under tini), so a bare `wine` is not
# on PATH at runtime. Report what IS available so the launcher can use it and so
# a failure is diagnosable from the install log.
echo "[refx][probe] locating a Proton/wine runner in the image (the yolk entrypoint that normally sets these up is bypassed)..."
for c in proton wine wine64 python3; do command -v "$c" >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "[refx][probe] on PATH: $(command -v "$c")"; done
find /home/container /usr /opt /root -maxdepth 6 -type f \( -name proton -o -name wine -o -name wine64 \) 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][probe] found runner: /' | head -20
find /home/container /usr /opt /root -maxdepth 4 -iname '*proton*' -type d 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][probe] proton dir: /' | head -10
echo "[refx][probe] done (if no runner is listed, this image ships none under the agent — the egg then needs Proton fetched in install, or a wine-capable image)."

# ---- Apply panel Startup-tab variables into PalWorldSettings.ini -----------
# The Windows build reads its ini from Config/WindowsServer (NOT LinuxServer).
# Palworld lets CLI flags override the ini and reads it only at boot, so we do
# NOT pass gameplay flags at launch; the panel writes the keys it owns here, at
# install/reinstall. Values are sanitized so they can't corrupt the single-line
# OptionSettings tuple. Everything else in the ini belongs to the customer.
INI_DIR=/mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/WindowsServer
INI="$INI_DIR/PalWorldSettings.ini"
mkdir -p "$INI_DIR"
if ! grep -q 'OptionSettings=(' "$INI" 2>/dev/null; then
  if [ -f /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
    cp /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini "$INI"
    echo "[refx] seeded PalWorldSettings.ini from DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini"
  else
    printf '[/Script/Pal.PalGameWorldSettings]\nOptionSettings=()\n' > "$INI"
  fi
fi
D=$'\x01'
sstr()  { printf '%s' "$1" | tr -d '"[:cntrl:]'; }
snum()  { printf '%s' "$1" | tr -cd '0-9'; }
sbool() { case "$(printf '%s' "$1" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')" in 1|true|yes|on) printf 'True';; *) printf 'False';; esac; }
esc()   { printf '%s' "$1" | sed -e 's/[\\&]/\\&/g'; }
set_opt() {
  key="$1"; rep="$(esc "$key=$2")"
  if grep -Eq "[(,][[:space:]]*$key=" "$INI"; then
    sed -i -E "s${D}([(,][[:space:]]*)$key=(\"[^\"]*\"|[^,)]*)${D}\1${rep}${D}" "$INI"
  elif grep -q 'OptionSettings=()' "$INI"; then
    sed -i "s${D}OptionSettings=()${D}OptionSettings=(${rep})${D}" "$INI"
  else
    sed -i "s${D}OptionSettings=(${D}OptionSettings=(${rep},${D}" "$INI"
  fi
}
[ -n "${SERVER_NAME:-}" ] && set_opt ServerName "\"$(sstr "$SERVER_NAME")\""
mp="$(snum "${MAX_PLAYERS:-}")"; [ -n "$mp" ] && set_opt ServerPlayerMaxNum "$mp"
if [ -n "${ADMIN_PASS:-}" ]; then set_opt AdminPassword "\"$(sstr "$ADMIN_PASS")\""; else echo "[refx] AdminPassword: Startup-tab field empty — leaving PalWorldSettings.ini untouched"; fi
set_opt RCONEnabled "$(sbool "${RCON_ENABLED:-true}")"
rp="$(snum "${RCON_PORT:-}")"; [ -n "$rp" ] && set_opt RCONPort "$rp"
gp="$(snum "${SERVER_PORT:-8211}")"; [ -n "$gp" ] && set_opt PublicPort "$gp"
echo "[refx] PalWorldSettings.ini (WindowsServer) synced (panel keys: ServerName, ServerPlayerMaxNum, RCONEnabled, RCONPort, PublicPort${ADMIN_PASS:+, AdminPassword}); all other settings read from the ini as saved."

# ---- Carry an existing world into the Windows server (world selection) ------
# Palworld's dedicated server loads the world named by DedicatedServerName in
# Config/WindowsServer/GameUserSettings.ini (section [/Script/Pal.PalGameLocalSettings]);
# it must equal a 32-hex folder under Pal/Saved/SaveGames/0/. If that pointer is
# absent or names a world that doesn't exist (e.g. after switching from the Linux
# egg, whose pointer lived in Config/LinuxServer), the server silently creates a
# NEW empty world instead of loading yours. So: if a real world is on disk but the
# Windows pointer is missing/invalid, point it at the largest Level.sav world (the
# real one, not an auto-generated empty). We only ever SET the pointer — never
# delete a save — and back the ini up first. .sav files are platform-identical.
SAVES=/mnt/server/Pal/Saved/SaveGames/0
WGUS=/mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/WindowsServer/GameUserSettings.ini
read_dsn() { [ -f "$1" ] && grep -oE '^DedicatedServerName=[0-9A-Fa-f]+' "$1" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | cut -d= -f2; }
if [ -d "$SAVES" ]; then
  # The REAL world = the folder with the LARGEST Level.sav. The empty world the
  # server auto-creates on first boot also has a (small) Level.sav, so we can't
  # trust "any valid pointer" — we must actively target the biggest one.
  best=""; best_sz=-1
  for d in "$SAVES"/*/; do
    [ -d "$d" ] || continue
    b="$(basename "$d")"
    case "$b" in *[!0-9A-Fa-f]*|"") continue;; esac
    [ ${#b} -eq 32 ] && [ -f "$d/Level.sav" ] || continue
    sz="$(stat -c%s "$d/Level.sav" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
    echo "[refx][world] candidate $b (Level.sav ${sz} bytes)"
    if [ "$sz" -gt "$best_sz" ]; then best_sz="$sz"; best="$b"; fi
  done
  if [ -z "$best" ]; then
    echo "[refx] no world with a Level.sav under SaveGames/0 yet — a fresh world is created on first boot."
  else
    cur="$(read_dsn "$WGUS")"
    if [ "$cur" = "$best" ]; then
      echo "[refx] world selection OK — DedicatedServerName=$best already targets your largest world."
    else
      # Point at the largest (real) world. Only SET the pointer; never touch saves.
      mkdir -p "$(dirname "$WGUS")"
      [ -f "$WGUS" ] && cp -a "$WGUS" "$WGUS.refxbak" 2>/dev/null || true
      if [ -f "$WGUS" ] && grep -q '^DedicatedServerName=' "$WGUS"; then
        sed -i -E "s/^DedicatedServerName=.*/DedicatedServerName=$best/" "$WGUS"
      elif [ -f "$WGUS" ] && grep -q '^\[/Script/Pal\.PalGameLocalSettings\]' "$WGUS"; then
        sed -i -E "/^\[\/Script\/Pal\.PalGameLocalSettings\]/a DedicatedServerName=$best" "$WGUS"
      else
        printf '[/Script/Pal.PalGameLocalSettings]\nDedicatedServerName=%s\n' "$best" >> "$WGUS"
      fi
      echo "[refx] pointed the Windows server at your largest world: DedicatedServerName=$best (Level.sav ${best_sz} bytes; was ${cur:-unset})$([ -f "$WGUS.refxbak" ] && printf '. Backup: %s' "$WGUS.refxbak")."
    fi
  fi
fi

# ---- Steam API runtime for the Windows server under Proton -----------------
# Palworld's server inits the Steam API (server list). Under Proton the Windows
# steam_api64.dll bridges to the native Linux steamclient.so via lsteamclient,
# which HARD-FAILS ("unable to load native steamclient library" + an assert that
# aborts the process) when steamclient.so is not reachable. Provide it in the
# locations Steam/Proton probe, from the steamcmd we bootstrapped, and drop
# steam_appid.txt so the API inits without a running Steam client. Adapts the
# arma3 egg's steamclient handling to the Proton bridge.
sc_find() { # $1 = 32|64 — first steamclient.so whose path names that arch
  find /mnt/server/steamcmd /home/container/Steam /home/container/.steam /root/.steam /root/.local /usr \
    2>/dev/null -type f -name steamclient.so \
    \( -path "*linux$1*" -o -path "*ubuntu12_$1*" -o -path "*sdk$1*" \) 2>/dev/null | head -n1
}
# Proton's lsteamclient dlopen()'s the native steamclient.so from a HARDCODED path:
# $HOME/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so — it IGNORES STEAM_COMPAT_CLIENT_INSTALL_PATH on
# Linux (ValveSoftware/Proton#9068). At runtime HOME=/home/container and the data
# volume is mounted THERE, so this MUST be written to the PERSISTED data dir
# (/mnt/server == /home/container at runtime) — writing to the install container's
# ephemeral /home/container would vanish before boot. Copy the whole steamcmd
# linux{64,32} dir so steamclient.so's sibling libs travel with it. Matches the
# official Pelican palworld-proton egg (stages linux64/steamclient.so -> sdk64).
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.steam/sdk32 /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64 /mnt/server/.steam/steam
SC64="$(sc_find 64)"; SC32="$(sc_find 32)"
if [ -n "$SC64" ] && [ -f "$SC64" ]; then
  cp -af "$(dirname "$SC64")/." /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64/ 2>/dev/null || cp -f "$SC64" /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so
  echo "[refx] steamclient.so (64-bit) staged into /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64 (== ~/.steam/sdk64 at runtime) from $(dirname "$SC64")"
else
  echo "[refx] WARN: 64-bit steamclient.so not found — the server WILL fail to init Steam (Proton#9068)."
fi
if [ -n "$SC32" ] && [ -f "$SC32" ]; then
  cp -af "$(dirname "$SC32")/." /mnt/server/.steam/sdk32/ 2>/dev/null || cp -f "$SC32" /mnt/server/.steam/sdk32/steamclient.so
fi
printf '2394010' > /mnt/server/steam_appid.txt 2>/dev/null || true
printf '2394010' > /mnt/server/Pal/Binaries/Win64/steam_appid.txt 2>/dev/null || true
echo "[refx] Steam API runtime prepared (steamclient.so -> ~/.steam/sdk{32,64} on the persisted volume; steam_appid.txt written)."

# ---- Write the ReFx launcher (startupCommand = "bash refx-run.sh") ---------
# The agent execs the startup command as ARGV under the image's tini entrypoint
# (splitArgs + direct exec) -- NOT through a shell -- so a raw startup line with
# an env-prefix / '&' / 'wait' is exec'd as a binary and fails. We run our own
# launcher instead (the proven arma3 pattern), which gives a real bash: the
# WINEDLLOVERRIDES env, background + log tail, and clean signal handling. The
# 'bash refx-*' name also lets the seed's migrateLauncherStartup heal existing
# servers' snapshotted startupCommand on the next re-seed.
cat > /mnt/server/refx-run.sh <<'RUNEOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
cd /home/container 2>/dev/null || cd "$(dirname "$0")"
# UE4SS injects through a dwmapi.dll PROXY next to the game exe. Force the NATIVE
# (our proxy) DLL first or UE4SS never loads. Proton/wine both honour this.
export WINEDLLOVERRIDES="dwmapi=n,b"
export WINEDEBUG="${WINEDEBUG:-fixme-all}"
EXE=./Pal/Binaries/Win64/PalServer-Win64-Shipping.exe
CONSOLE=./Pal/refx-console.log
GAMELOG=./Pal/Saved/Logs/Pal.log
PORT="${SERVER_PORT:-8211}"

echo "[refx] launcher starting (pwd=$PWD)."
command -v xvfb-run >/dev/null 2>&1 || echo "[refx] WARNING: xvfb-run is not on PATH."
[ -f "$EXE" ] && echo "[refx] server exe present." || echo "[refx] ERROR: $EXE is missing."
[ -f ./Pal/Binaries/Win64/dwmapi.dll ] && echo "[refx] UE4SS dwmapi.dll present." || echo "[refx] WARNING: dwmapi.dll missing (UE4SS will not load)."

# The steamcmd:proton image has NO bare `wine`/`proton` on PATH under the agent
# (the yolk's entrypoint, which we bypass, sets those up). Locate a runner
# ourselves: prefer Proton (`proton run`), fall back to a wine/wine64 binary.
PROTON=""; WINEBIN=""
for p in "$(command -v proton 2>/dev/null)" \
         /home/container/GE-Proton*/proton \
         /home/container/.steam/steam/compatibilitytools.d/*/proton \
         /home/container/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton*/proton \
         /usr/local/bin/proton /opt/proton*/proton; do
  [ -n "${p:-}" ] && [ -f "$p" ] && { PROTON="$p"; break; }
done
for w in "$(command -v wine64 2>/dev/null)" "$(command -v wine 2>/dev/null)" \
         /home/container/GE-Proton*/files/bin/wine64 \
         /home/container/GE-Proton*/files/bin/wine \
         /usr/lib/wine/wine64 /opt/wine*/bin/wine64 \
         /usr/local/bin/wine64 /usr/local/bin/wine; do
  [ -n "${w:-}" ] && [ -f "$w" ] && { WINEBIN="$w"; break; }
done
if [ -z "$PROTON" ] && [ -z "$WINEBIN" ]; then
  echo "[refx] no runner in the usual spots — searching the image..."
  found="$(find /home/container /usr /opt /root -maxdepth 6 -type f \( -name proton -o -name wine64 -o -name wine \) 2>/dev/null | head -n1)"
  case "${found:-}" in
    */proton) PROTON="$found";;
    */wine*)  WINEBIN="$found";;
  esac
fi
if [ -z "$PROTON" ] && [ -z "$WINEBIN" ]; then
  echo "[refx] FATAL: no Proton or wine runner found in this image — the Windows server cannot be launched."
  echo "[refx][diag] /usr/local/bin:"; ls -la /usr/local/bin 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head -40
  echo "[refx][diag] /home/container:"; ls -la /home/container 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head -40
  echo "[refx][diag] anything proton/wine:"; find /home/container /usr /opt /root -maxdepth 6 \( -iname '*proton*' -o -iname '*wine*' \) 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head -30
  exit 1
fi

SRV=
shutdown() { [ -n "$SRV" ] && kill -TERM "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; command -v wineserver >/dev/null 2>&1 && wineserver -k 2>/dev/null || true; }
trap shutdown TERM INT

: > "$CONSOLE"
if [ -n "$PROTON" ]; then
  echo "[refx] using Proton runner: $PROTON"
  export HOME="${HOME:-/home/container}"
  export STEAM_COMPAT_CLIENT_INSTALL_PATH="${STEAM_COMPAT_CLIENT_INSTALL_PATH:-/home/container/.steam/steam}"
  export STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH="${STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH:-/home/container/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/2394010}"
  export STEAM_COMPAT_APP_ID=2394010 SteamAppId=2394010 SteamGameId=2394010
  mkdir -p "$STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH" "$STEAM_COMPAT_CLIENT_INSTALL_PATH"
  # Proton's lsteamclient dlopen()'s $HOME/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so (hardcoded,
  # Proton#9068). Verify it is there, or Steam init aborts the boot.
  if [ -f "$HOME/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so" ]; then
    echo "[refx] steamclient.so present at $HOME/.steam/sdk64 — Steam bridge should init."
  else
    echo "[refx] WARNING: $HOME/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so is MISSING — Steam init will fail; reinstall to stage it."
  fi
  echo "[refx] launching via Proton: $EXE Pal -port=$PORT"
  xvfb-run -a "$PROTON" run "$EXE" Pal -port="$PORT" -useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS > "$CONSOLE" 2>&1 &
  SRV=$!
else
  echo "[refx] using wine runner: $WINEBIN"
  export WINEARCH=win64
  export WINEPREFIX="${WINEPREFIX:-/home/container/.wine}"
  export PATH="$(dirname "$WINEBIN"):$PATH"
  if [ ! -f "$WINEPREFIX/system.reg" ]; then
    echo "[refx] initializing wine prefix at $WINEPREFIX (first boot; can take a minute)..."
    xvfb-run -a "$WINEBIN" wineboot --init >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    command -v wineserver >/dev/null 2>&1 && wineserver -w 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  echo "[refx] launching via wine: $EXE Pal -port=$PORT"
  xvfb-run -a "$WINEBIN" "$EXE" Pal -port="$PORT" -useperfthreads -NoAsyncLoadingThread -UseMultithreadForDS > "$CONSOLE" 2>&1 &
  SRV=$!
fi
# Stream the runner console + game log + UE4SS log so nothing is lost.
tail -n +1 -F "$CONSOLE" "$GAMELOG" ./Pal/Binaries/Win64/ue4ss/UE4SS.log 2>/dev/null &

while kill -0 "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; do wait "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; done
RC=$?
echo "[refx] PalServer exited (code $RC)."
echo "[refx] --- last 80 lines of the runner console ---"
tail -n 80 "$CONSOLE" 2>/dev/null
echo "[refx] (no output + instant exit usually means a missing runtime dependency or a headless/Proton init failure; this egg is experimental.)"
RUNEOF
chmod +x /mnt/server/refx-run.sh 2>/dev/null || true
echo "[refx] wrote refx-run.sh launcher (startup runs: bash refx-run.sh)."

echo 'Palworld (Windows/UE4SS, experimental) install complete.'

variables.description: Name shown in the in-game server browser. Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (ServerName) under Config/WindowsServer at install/reinstall.
Maximum concurrent players (hard cap of 32). Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (ServerPlayerMaxNum) at install/reinstall.

Password for in-game admin commands and RCON. When set, it is written into PalWorldSettings.ini (AdminPassword) at install/reinstall; leave blank to manage it directly in the ini. To change it later, update this field and Reinstall (preserves your world), or edit the ini in the file manager while stopped.
Enable the remote console interface. Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (RCONEnabled) at install/reinstall.
TCP port for the RCON interface (allocated by the panel and written into PalWorldSettings.ini at install/reinstall).

longDescription: EXPERIMENTAL Windows Palworld dedicated server (Proton/Wine) with RE-UE4SS bootstrapped for server-side mods. Deploy to a single test server to confirm a real server-side mod loads before building the mod manager. Palworld updates routinely break UE4SS; Pocketpair considers server mods experimental.
